name: april 8th
content: Once again I am attempting to alleviate an anxiety pang by writing. I heave heavy and churn my chest. Twenty minutes in the moon has yet to have fixed shit. It is two forty three in the morning. I could not sleep right now if I tried; I am awake like a sort of dysfunctional Kraken with a fear of change, like a basket case. I drink to aid the mental illness—I call it an illness because it warrants admittance. A padawan on Sidechat said, recently, that “Once we have spoken our saddest story, we can be free of it”. I have my unfair share of sad stories, yet I refuse to call myself one yet. I have quite a bit of a ways to go, I’d like to think. It is, after all, a young Starry Night. It is cold. It is also empty outside. Some snow made its way here last night and the one before, but under the wrath of the Sun most of it seems to be molten; there are some splotches here and there and a light frosting layer on windshields, but this is normal. You may have noticed by now that there is really no coherence to this piece of writing, and I’d like to remind you that the purpose—primarily—of this exercise is self-serving and that any byproducts are byproducts thereof.

There is much to do. There is much I want to do, really. There’s a war sort of ongoing, the chief exec of Air India just resigned, the Indian Ready-to-Drink (ABV 8% to 15%) market is a case prime in its applicability to extrapolation. I miss Lay’s onion-ish chips, the ones shrouded in green. Sour Cream & Onion: there it is. It is now two fifty three and I feel pretty shit still. Even if, however, writing isn’t a causality to bliss, I am more than okay with recording this process simply for archival’s sakes.

As you go on—as I go on—this may start to make some sense. Bear with me as I have a lot to say and it is all mixed up and jumbled in my head. Think of it like multicolored wires to a bomb: red, green, blue, you get it. The disentanglement hereof is my peril. To be clear, I have no intentions of reopening the Strait of Hormuz tonight. If that does happen, though, it will be a welcome artefact of what I will retrospectively label boredom for the sake of whatever performance I am putting up then. I’m nearly always performing, as—I believe—are all of us. When I say us, though, I’m not sure who I refer to. In writing the narrator tends to appeal to some collectivistic sense of normality; I could be referring to us humans, us Americans[1], us writers, us anything, really. Us is thus seen most commonly as a mirrored construction of the self—a sort of projection, if you will. I juxtapose our shared understanding of ourselves against very many sets of others, whom you will meet soon. I am writing this deliberately and against an instinct as a nonsensical rant, as I am told that it helps to be organic more than it does to be organized. The organs are in overdrive either way.

Nights like these I often think back to my Model UN days. In my second-ever conference I represented Syria, which was then under the Assad regime. Even as a seventh grader I was acutely aware that they’d done some fucked up shit, and that I was to defend it against unnamed hegemons presenting themselves as ‘Delegates of’ fuck knows where. I sometimes miss those days, when I cosplayed control. I think sometimes of what I’d do if I were in that place today. In many ways, after all, I am. We have moral ambiguity at home, and we have diluted conscience. We have complicity, and we have enablement. I pay taxes to a regime that is hell-bent on “fire and fury”, yet I seem to speak in its opposition. This is not an entirely sound argument, of course; “you criticize the system yet you are part of it” and “if there’s a fire you’re trying to douse you can’t put it out from inside the house” (Miranda 2015) are both effectively the same falsifiable motif.

I also want to give a sort of honorary mention—a shoutout, even—to British exchange students. I mean this descriptor to say students from universities in the United Kingdom—there is much conflation herein but technicalities aside you know who I’m talking about by simply hearing them speak; they stand out. We are now nearly eight hundred words in and I haven’t gotten anywhere, but honestly I consider pointless housewarmings a compliment to writing since waffling is much preferred to blankets of blankness. I also think that I tell you a lot when I don’t tell you anything per se, but not in a ‘get to know me’ sense. The inability to breathe has returned. I am again straining my upper chest like a thread that’s pulled too tight and then slowly untensed before the point of breakage.

I should get up and do something. I’m not quite sure what, but I know that I’m going to end up smoking weed in the stairwell. Stairwells are special places to me for reasons good, bad, and ugly. Iconic ones, pre-college, include that located at the aft entrance of Scottish High International School’s auditorium, that next to my house, and (albeit less remarkably) third spaces. The third place thesis, too, holds a special place in my heart. I ‘came up’ with it when I first did magic mushrooms in the Spring of 2025 but never really went anywhere with it; this is because the smokeshop guys are terribly inconsistent and at the time ceased sourcing the weed of eternal wisdom and truth. Instead, we ended up with mid weed, which was honestly better still than the weed of lies and deceit. “Weed is weed”, yes, but snobbery is underrated by the masses in my opinion. There is some truth beyond bullshittery to the art of wine tasting, for instance, as there is to eating a woman out.

Three eighteen and one thousand words. Almost done with this bottle of Bro Code, which is a 15% aerated wine sold in the beer form factor. Interestingly it is a different vibe from wine drunk, but that’s probably mostly down to me falling for a marketing gimmick. That does not make it—in the slightest—any less interesting, though. I also think often about Barron Trump. I am semi-chronically online and refresh my Threads feed at least ten times a day, and I am also locked into my echo chamber. We smoke lots of weed, we shit on ‘herr administration’[2], and we are predominantly wannabe-Black. Again, I really don’t know how I ended up on Black or quasi-performative-Black Threads, but I am here now.

With social media the thing I find peculiar is that we did have focal points not very long ago. Twitter splintered out into Threads and Xitter and Bluesky and Mastodon and Truth Social and whatnot only after Musk, and we know that this is anecdotally somewhat valid since Prof. Jura Liaukonyte, who teaches Advertising and Pricing at the Dyson School[3], left Xitter shortly after the incelistic takeover.

Modern internet theory really is sort of fascinating. @etymologynerd is someone I look at as a good example of an internet theorist; his methods really are not very scientific but I will give him a break because this is an instance of a white man using his platform for objectivity. See? I said that in a very ‘Black Threads’ sort of way, because how the fuck does it matter that he’s white? I don’t think it does, but I do think it impacts his scientific perspective; after all, he’ll never understand AAVE[4] the way a quintessential Detroit barber would.

Detroit is probably not a place we should talk about. My friend [Asfi](#/work/tias) is currently back home in Detroit not three days after the conclusion of Spring Break because her Dad had a heart attack. He is okay right now and he will most likely live, so I’m not a complete asshole for testifying to her absences right now[5]. This is also my personal piece of writing so any standards of morality can sort of fuck right off unless previously declared superlative by my very self. I do find it liberating that I get to set my own systems and limits. I also find it sort of overwhelming that I’m in charge of my life. Was Sisyphus happy? Fuck if I know, and I did try to write about this about a year and a half ago. I began working on a book called “A Semi-Colon in History;” and never really took it anywhere despite its having promise. My writing head came to a block soon after I wrote [“A Murkiness in Bangkok”](#/work/a-murkiness-in-bangkok) in early January of 2025. A friend of mine—a best friend I called him—broke my left forearm shortly after I was done with the piece, but that is more of a misnomerity than it is a reason.

I am anxious tonight about lots of things, as is normal, but in particular I am stressed about my job. I am a resident advisor—an RA—at an Ivy League university. The latter label does not hold too much meaning to me in my current state but that is purely out of a narcissistic privilege. Bro Code is finally over, by the way. We are now onto “Shotgun” by Fratelli, which is actually a vineyard-owning company in India. Fratelli has tapped well into this RTD market, which is characterized by a high degree of substitutability. “Alcohol is alcohol is alcohol” is the warchant mantra for this group of consumers, who cumulatively represent quite a fat demographic, especially in ‘frontier’ markets. I was actually part of the Emerging Markets Society (now the Emerging Markets Institute Club) before that idea went to shit, the idea being my involvement therein, not the club itself. My job (I am[6] an RA! Fuck yeah!) requires me to attend a fuck ass weekly meeting that conflicts directly with the club. Fuck me, I know.

This is a weirdly solvable inefficiency, by the way, as are many others at Cornell. Student Assembly meets Thursdays at like half past four or something; this has been their schedule since forever. University Assembly has a similar sort of regimen for Wednesdays and so does Housing[7]. The whole point of these ‘schedules’ is predictability but alas courtship is lost on the lawless. This aphorism I would like to place amidst a text slightly more meaningful and quite a bit more comprehensible than this one, but I am still looking to my head to see when it decides to get its fucking shit together. I should get my shit together, and I want to, but I haven’t done this yet. I should really get to it; there’s too many sets of eyes peering at the corner of the wall to see my arm and gun pop out behind it and start rattling bullets off. I will now go and take a piss since we’ve hit nineteen fifty one and three forty one, neither of which are good things. I also do feel like shitting, but I don’t know if this is a biological or a psychological thing. I am burping like a madman; this is down to the alcohol. If you find it insufferable, know that I find it twenty times so. I also dislike the use of arbitrary numbers like “a hundred and ten [percent]”. See? Another thing you know ahout me. We’re at 2030!
Narrator: At this point, the aforementioned pang had been, presumably, alleviated.
footnotes: [1] This is as much hyperbole as it is not.
[2] See ‘Herr Doktor’, as in Lady Lazarus (Plath 1965): “So, so, Herr Doktor. [LINE BREAK] So, Herr Enemy.”
[3] And, whom I love very much [ADDITION MADE 070926]
[7] When I say ‘Housing’ or ‘Big Housing’ I am referring to the powers that be. This is not an honorific. It is quite horrific, rather.
